resources.string: The Control center performs the elevated actions the Manager can do itself when you grant it Shizuku. Everything here is optional and reversible-first: it suspends rather than uninstalls, and every appops or permission change can be undone. Nothing here runs unless you have granted a privileged shell.

These actions run through the Shizuku shell you optionally grant the Manager. Grant it in the Elevation center (Tools tab) and these controls light up. Until then they stay off — the Manager never fakes a privileged action.
Shizuku shell granted — the actions below are live.

Suspend a set of installed apps at once (greyed out, cannot launch). This is reversible: un-suspend the same set with one tap. Suite apps, your launcher, Settings, Tailscale, and the Shizuku manager are always excluded — they are never suspended.
Tick the apps to lock down. Excluded system, suite, and connectivity apps are not shown. Suspending is fully reversible.
Also force-stop each app when suspending
Select at least one app to lock down.
Nothing is locked down right now.
%1$d app(s) locked down. They stay saved here so you can restore them even after relaunch.
No eligible apps to lock down were found.

Pick an installed app to review its sensitive grants — overlay, camera, microphone, location, usage access, and dangerous runtime permissions. Revoke a permission or set an appop to ignore in one tap, with undo. Accessibility and device-admin are handled in system Settings.
A privileged read was not available, so this shows the rootless permission view from the package manager. Grant Shizuku for the deeper appops read.
No sensitive grants were detected for this app.
This app holds an accessibility service. Accessibility is system-gated — review and turn it off in Settings.
This app is an active device administrator. Remove device-admin in Settings before it can be uninstalled.

Force DNS-over-TLS to a specific host across the whole device. This complements the Automatic/Off buttons in the Elevation center. The Manager reads the live Private DNS state back so you see exactly what was applied.
Enter a hostname to apply strict Private DNS.
Current Private DNS state could not be read.

Enrich the rootless sweep with a few extra reads from the privileged shell: doze/idle state, overlay and usage-access appops, background-network restrictions, and lock-screen settings. These are additive — if a read is unavailable it is simply skipped.
No extra posture rows were available from the privileged shell.
